name: whisperbot
description: Telegram bot that transcribes audio messages via whisper.cpp
readme_summary: Whisperbot is a Telegram bot written in C that transcribes audio messages into text using either whisper.cpp or qwen-asr as the backend. It handles audio conversion, transcription processing, and streams the text back to the user, with options for managing queue length and model selection for performance.
key_features: Transcribes audio messages from Telegram using whisper.cpp or qwen-asr
Handles audio downloading and conversion to WAV
Streams transcription results back to Telegram as they are generated
Manages ASR processing queue to balance latency and throughput
Dynamically switches between faster and better ASR models based on queue length
Configurable limits for queue size, audio duration, and message length
primary_use_case: Transcribing Telegram voice messages into text automatically.
cover_image_prompt: A friendly robot assistant in a Telegram chat interface, holding a microphone and a speech bubble. The microphone's sound waves are visibly transforming into clean, readable text within the speech bubble. Behind the robot, a subtle background shows a queue of incoming audio messages being processed efficiently. Small, glowing icons representing 'whisper.cpp' and 'qwen-asr' are visible on the robot's chassis. The overall mood is helpful and efficient, with a clean, modern aesthetic and soft, digital lighting. The image should be a 3D isometric illustration with clear visual metaphors for audio-to-text conversion and queue management.

name: FireRedVAD
description: A SOTA Industrial-Grade Voice Activity Detection & Audio Event Detection, supporting 100+ languages, outperforming Silero-VAD, TEN-VAD, FunASR-VAD and WebRTC-VAD
readme_summary: FireRedVAD is a state-of-the-art Voice Activity Detection (VAD) and Audio Event Detection (AED) solution. It supports over 100 languages for speech, singing, and music detection, offering both streaming and non-streaming capabilities. The system achieves high accuracy, outperforming existing VAD solutions on benchmark datasets.
key_features: State-of-the-art Voice Activity Detection (VAD)
Industrial-grade Audio Event Detection (AED)
Supports 100+ languages
Detects speech, singing, and music
Supports non-streaming and streaming VAD
Supports non-streaming AED
Outperforms existing VAD solutions (Silero-VAD, TEN-VAD, FunASR-VAD, WebRTC-VAD)
primary_use_case: Accurate detection of voice activity and specific audio events in various languages for applications requiring audio analysis.
cover_image_prompt: A highly detailed 3D isometric illustration of a sound engineer's control room. In the center, a large, glowing audio waveform is being meticulously dissected by a sophisticated, multi-lens magnifying glass. On one side of the magnifying glass, the waveform is clearly segmented into distinct 'speech', 'singing', and 'music' sections, each highlighted with a different vibrant color. On the other side, a stream of digital particles representing raw audio data flows into the magnifying glass, and precise timestamps and event labels emerge as the output. Subtle UI elements displaying 'FireRedVAD' and real-time F1 scores are visible on a nearby monitor. The overall mood is precise and analytical, with a clean, futuristic aesthetic and a cool blue and orange color palette.

name: cloudflare-solver
description: Cloudflare Challenge TurnStile Solver. Asynchronous Python solution for bypassing Cloudflare's anti-bot turnstile challenges and obtaining the cf_clearance cookie. With humanization and proxy support.
readme_summary: This Python repository provides an asynchronous solution for bypassing Cloudflare's anti-bot challenges, specifically Turnstile and Challenge types. It aims to automate the process of obtaining necessary cookies or tokens, supporting features like proxy usage and realistic browser fingerprinting for stealth.
key_features: Dual Challenge Support (Challenge and Turnstile)
Asynchronous operation using asyncio and Playwright
Stealthy browser fingerprinting with Camoufox and BrowserForge
Modular OOP design
Configurable options (retries, delays, OS)
Built-in logging
Proxy support
Humanization features
primary_use_case: Automating the bypassing of Cloudflare's anti-bot challenges to obtain `cf_clearance` cookies or Turnstile tokens for programmatic access to Cloudflare-protected websites.
cover_image_prompt: A digital locksmith, depicted as a sleek, modern robot with glowing blue accents, skillfully picking a complex, multi-layered lock representing Cloudflare's security. The lock has intricate gears and digital circuits. As the locksmith turns the key, a golden `cf_clearance` cookie and a glowing token emerge from the lock. In the background, a subtle, abstract representation of a web browser window shows a progress bar filling up. The scene is set against a dark, futuristic backdrop with faint lines of code and data streams. The overall style is a 3D isometric illustration with a clean, high-tech aesthetic, emphasizing precision and automation.

name: netsnap
description: CLI utility for switching network profiles on Windows — one command instead of 7 clicks
readme_summary: netsnap is a command-line utility for Windows that simplifies switching between pre-configured network profiles. It allows users to save and apply different network settings (IP address, gateway, DNS) with a single command, eliminating the need for manual configuration through the GUI.
key_features: CLI utility for network profile switching on Windows
Store network configurations as named profiles in JSON
Apply profiles with a single command (e.g., `netsnap apply work`)
Supports static IP, DHCP, gateway, and DNS settings
Uses `netsh` for applying settings and WMI for reading current state
Requires administrator privileges to modify network settings
primary_use_case: Quickly and easily switching between different network configurations on a Windows machine, such as moving between office, home, and on-site network environments, without manual GUI interaction.
cover_image_prompt: A busy IT technician, depicted as a conductor, standing in front of a large, complex soundboard. Each knob and slider on the soundboard represents a different network profile (e.g., 'Office', 'Home', 'Site'). With a swift, elegant gesture of their baton, the conductor adjusts the soundboard, instantly changing the visual flow of data streams represented by glowing fiber optic cables connected to various devices. Small, subtle holographic displays around the technician show simplified network icons and the repository name 'netsnap'. The overall mood is one of effortless control and efficiency, rendered in a clean, modern 3D isometric illustration style with a vibrant, tech-focused color palette.

name: retroassembly
description: The personal retro game collection cabinet in your browser. Join us on Discord https://discord.gg/gwaKRAYG6t
readme_summary: RetroAssembly is a web-based personal retro game collection cabinet that allows users to play classic games directly in their browser. It features a visually appealing library display with auto-detected box arts, save/load functionality, rewind capabilities, and intuitive spatial navigation for browsing games.
key_features: Play numerous retro gaming consoles (NES, SNES, Genesis, GameBoy, Arcade, etc.) in the browser.
Visually browse game collection with auto-detected box arts.
Save and synchronize game progress.
Rewind gameplay (where supported by emulator).
Intuitive spatial navigation using keyboard or gamepad.
Retro-style shaders for enhanced visuals.
On-screen virtual controller for touch devices.
primary_use_case: Playing retro video games in a web browser with a curated collection interface.
cover_image_prompt: A vibrant, stylized illustration of a classic arcade cabinet reimagined as a modern web browser window. Inside the browser window, a user's hand is reaching out, holding a retro gamepad, about to select a game from a beautifully organized shelf of glowing game cartridges. Each cartridge displays its iconic box art. Subtle digital particles flow from the screen, hinting at the emulation technology. The background is a soft, abstract representation of a digital library. The overall mood is nostalgic yet futuristic, rendered in a 3D isometric illustration style with a focus on clean lines and bright, inviting colors. The repository name 'RetroAssembly' is subtly integrated into the arcade cabinet's marquee.
